proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of marginal dose-response derivatives under bounded unmeasured confounding via a continuous instrumental variable with quantile-copula restrictions on potential-outcome dependence
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue material remains doc/API.md §3, which records ThmSmith/PartialID as an empty seed area, and the banked pid_dose_response_shape proposal, whose kernel is monotone-concave closure of marginal continuous-dose sensitivity bands without an IV, adjacent-rank copula, or baseline-rank derivative target. The current proposal is therefore not a relabelling of an in-repo entry: its novelty axis is joint sharpness and sign certification for an adjacent-rank slope under IV-compatible margins.","published_axis":"The v5 patch adds Zhang2025ADE as the closest derivative-target comparator: Zhang bounds the average derivative effect under a generalized-propensity odds-ratio sensitivity model. The gap is that an ADE bound averages over the exposure distribution and does not condition on a pinned baseline potential-outcome rank, nor does it use continuous-IV moment information and an adjacent-rank copula tube to rule out sign-reversing cross-dose couplings. BaitairianSebastienJreichKatsahianGuilloux2025 remains closest for sharp APO sensitivity bounds, and ChernozhukovFernandezValHanWuthrich2024 remains closest for IV/copula identification, but neither subsumes the rank-slice partial-ID slope-sign kernel."}
seed_list: Prior work Kasy2014 would predict that continuous-IV support mainly governs marginal potential-outcome distributions. Our seed conjectures that local derivative signs can be certified even when marginal mean-slope bands straddle zero, because an adjacent-dose rank-copula tube blocks adversarial high-low rank pairings.

Prior work FirpoRidder2019 establishes that pointwise Makarov bounds are not uniformly sharp for process functionals. Our seed conjectures an analogous nonuniformity for continuous-dose finite differences, because two dosewise sharp sensitivity adversaries cannot always be pasted through one adjacent-rank coupling.

Prior work BaitairianSebastienJreichKatsahianGuilloux2025 establishes pointwise sharp APO bounds under a continuous sensitivity model. Our seed conjectures that pointwise sharp APO bounds cease to be jointly sharp for rank-slice slopes once one copula tube must rationalize adjacent doses.

Prior work DalalTchetgenTchetgen2025 bounds endogenous continuous-treatment dose-response curves under sensitivity models. Our seed conjectures that a continuous IV plus quantile-copula tube creates a derivative sign certificate invisible to their global dose-response envelope.

Prior work BaiocchiChengSmall2023 assumes continuous-IV sensitivity models must use dose information. Our seed conjectures that rank information, not only dose magnitude, is necessary: dose-aware sensitivity still permits false local derivative ambiguity unless adjacent potential-outcome ranks are coupled.

Prior work MadigasekaraPoskittZhangZhao2024 uses copula equality to tighten panel distributional treatment-effect bounds. Our seed conjectures that equality is stronger than needed for continuous-dose local slopes, because a bounded adjacent-rank tube already creates a sharp sign phase transition.

Prior work Torgovitsky2015 would suggest scalar heterogeneity restrictions are the route from small-support instruments to point identification. Our seed conjectures that without scalar point ID, a quantile-copula tube can still identify the sign of a rank-slice derivative.

Prior work DHaultfoeuilleFevrier2015 establishes point ID under triangular monotonicity and support/intersection conditions. Our seed conjectures that when those conditions fail, local derivative sign can remain sharply partially identified through copula-constrained coupling.

Prior work Kim2020 shows outcome-shape restrictions add identifying power in nonseparable IV models. Our seed conjectures that dependence-shape restrictions add a different power: rank-copula geometry tightens local slopes without imposing concavity or monotonicity of the dose-response curve.

Prior work MarmarelisHaddadJessonJahanshadGalstyanVerSteeg2023 bounds continuous-dose responses under hidden confounding. Our seed conjectures their dosewise hidden-confounder envelopes hide a stricter cross-dose compatibility constraint when the target is a rank-slice derivative.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Kasy2014 would predict that continuous-IV support mainly governs marginal potential-outcome distributions. Our seed conjectures that local derivative signs can be certified even when marginal mean-slope bands straddle zero, because an adjacent-dose rank-copula tube blocks adversarial high-low rank pairings.

anchor_paper.author: Kasy
anchor_paper.claim: Continuous IVs identify or partially identify potential-outcome distributions depending on support richness.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: the new phenomenon is sign certification from rank coupling, not merely weaker support.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Kasy2014 because derivatives of the average structural function are determined by identified marginals.
seed_details.rebuttal: False for the rank-slice slope target: Theorem 2 in the proposal explicitly separates full-population mean slopes from copula-dependent rank-slice slopes.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work FirpoRidder2019 establishes that pointwise Makarov bounds are not uniformly sharp for process functionals. Our seed conjectures an analogous nonuniformity for continuous-dose finite differences, because two dosewise sharp sensitivity adversaries cannot always be pasted through one adjacent-rank coupling.

anchor_paper.author: Firpo and Ridder
anchor_paper.claim: Makarov bounds are pointwise but not uniformly sharp for treatment-effect-distribution functionals.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist; it transfers a nonuniform sharpness phenomenon to a new continuous-IV derivative object with a copula tube.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from FirpoRidder2019 because finite differences are treatment-effect distributions with known marginals.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the proposal adds continuous-IV sensitivity envelopes and adjacent-dose rank tubes, not just known binary potential-outcome marginals.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BaitairianSebastienJreichKatsahianGuilloux2025 establishes pointwise sharp APO bounds under a continuous sensitivity model. Our seed conjectures that pointwise sharp APO bounds cease to be jointly sharp for rank-slice slopes once one copula tube must rationalize adjacent doses.

anchor_paper.author: Baitairian et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp APO bounds are available under a continuous sensitivity model.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Blocked pattern would be CATE/derivative of APO, but not blocked because the derivative target exposes a joint-pasting gap hidden by pointwise APO sharpness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from their sharp APO theorem because slope bounds are differences of APO bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: False for rank-slice slopes and joint sharpness: separately optimized APO endpoints need not share a feasible copula or latent law.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DalalTchetgenTchetgen2025 bounds endogenous continuous-treatment dose-response curves under sensitivity models. Our seed conjectures that a continuous IV plus quantile-copula tube creates a derivative sign certificate invisible to their global dose-response envelope.

anchor_paper.author: Dalal and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Bounds counterfactual dose-response curves for endogenous continuous treatments under Rosenbaum and marginal sensitivity models.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends method to IV would be blocked, but not blocked because the proposed effect is a sign-certification phase transition created by copula coupling.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an incremental IV add-on to their continuous-treatment sensitivity bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposal targets cross-dose rank-slice derivative signs; their object is a dose-response envelope and does not contain the copula sign phase transition.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BaiocchiChengSmall2023 assumes continuous-IV sensitivity models must use dose information. Our seed conjectures that rank information, not only dose magnitude, is necessary: dose-aware sensitivity still permits false local derivative ambiguity unless adjacent potential-outcome ranks are coupled.

anchor_paper.author: Baiocchi, Cheng, and Small
anchor_paper.claim: A valid sensitivity model for continuous IVs must incorporate continuous dose information.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist; the mechanism is rank information versus dose information.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from their theorem because dose-aware sensitivity already fixes the continuous-IV problem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their theorem concerns validity of sensitivity models for IV dose assignment, not cross-dose potential-outcome rank dependence or derivative sharpness.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MadigasekaraPoskittZhangZhao2024 uses copula equality to tighten panel distributional treatment-effect bounds. Our seed conjectures that equality is stronger than needed for continuous-dose local slopes, because a bounded adjacent-rank tube already creates a sharp sign phase transition.

anchor_paper.author: Madigasekara et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Copula equality assumptions tighten panel distributional treatment-effect bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends method from panel to continuous dose would be blocked, but not blocked because the claim is a weaker tube restriction with a new sign phase transition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a direct continuous-dose analogue of panel copula equality bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the proposed restriction is an adjacent-dose rank tube, not equality over time, and the target is a local slope under IV sensitivity.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Torgovitsky2015 would suggest scalar heterogeneity restrictions are the route from small-support instruments to point identification. Our seed conjectures that without scalar point ID, a quantile-copula tube can still identify the sign of a rank-slice derivative.

anchor_paper.author: Torgovitsky
anchor_paper.claim: Scalar heterogeneity restrictions can identify continuous-treatment response distributions with small-support instruments.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens assumptions would be blocked, but not blocked because the proposed output is not point ID; it is a sign-only partial-ID phase transition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from scalar-heterogeneity point identification by weakening the conclusion.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposal operates when scalar point-ID conditions are not imposed and uses copula tubes, a different identifying mechanism.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DHaultfoeuilleFevrier2015 establishes point ID under triangular monotonicity and support/intersection conditions. Our seed conjectures that when those conditions fail, local derivative sign can remain sharply partially identified through copula-constrained coupling.

anchor_paper.author: D'Haultfoeuille and Fevrier
anchor_paper.claim: Nonseparable triangular models can be point identified with discrete instruments under monotonicity/intersection conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: the seed asks for a residual sign-identification boundary after point-ID conditions fail.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just partial identification when their point-identification condition fails.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed names a specific residual identifying mechanism, copula-constrained local rank slopes, not generic partial-ID fallback.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Kim2020 shows outcome-shape restrictions add identifying power in nonseparable IV models. Our seed conjectures that dependence-shape restrictions add a different power: rank-copula geometry tightens local slopes without imposing concavity or monotonicity of the dose-response curve.

anchor_paper.author: Kim
anchor_paper.claim: Monotonicity and concavity provide identifying power in binary-IV nonseparable models when point-ID conditions fail.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Generalizes shape restrictions would be blocked, but not blocked because the shape is on dependence geometry, not the outcome response function, and it yields a sign-certificate boundary.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from shape-restricted partial identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: Outcome-response concavity and rank-copula tubes constrain different objects and produce different finite optimization problems.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MarmarelisHaddadJessonJahanshadGalstyanVerSteeg2023 bounds continuous-dose responses under hidden confounding. Our seed conjectures their dosewise hidden-confounder envelopes hide a stricter cross-dose compatibility constraint when the target is a rank-slice derivative.

anchor_paper.author: Marmarelis et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Bounds average and conditional average continuous-valued treatment effects under hidden confounding.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Derives derivative version of dose-response bounds would be blocked, but not blocked because the derivative target exposes joint compatibility of dosewise adversaries.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the slope of their dose-response bound.
seed_details.rebuttal: The rank-slice slope is not the slope of an average curve, and the copula-pasting condition is absent from their dosewise envelope.

proposed_from.literature_map: Kasy2014 (Review of Economic Studies, https://doi.org/10.1093/restud/rdu018) identifies or partially identifies potential-outcome distributions with continuous instruments; open gap is local derivative functionals that depend on cross-dose potential-outcome dependence. Torgovitsky2015 (Econometrica, https://doi.org/10.3982/ECTA9984) and DHaultfoeuilleFevrier2015 (Econometrica) show scalar-heterogeneity triangular restrictions can restore point identification with small-support instruments; tension is whether weaker copula restrictions can certify signs without point ID. Kim2020 (Econometric Theory) shows binary-instrument nonseparable models remain partially informative under shape restrictions; open gap is rank-copula shape rather than outcome-response shape. JessonDouglasManshausenSolalMeinshausenStierGalShalit2022 (NeurIPS) and MarmarelisHaddadJessonJahanshadGalstyanVerSteeg2023 (AAAI) provide continuous-treatment hidden-confounding dose-response bounds; tension is dosewise adversaries versus one joint potential-outcome process. BaitairianSebastienJreichKatsahianGuilloux2025 (Biometrical Journal) gives sharp APO bounds under continuous sensitivity, but pointwise sharpness need not be joint derivative sharpness. DalalTchetgenTchetgen2025 (arXiv, https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.13946) bounds endogenous continuous-treatment dose-response curves under Rosenbaum/marginal sensitivity; gap is IV plus copula-restricted local slopes. FirpoRidder2019 (Journal of Econometrics, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2019.04.012) shows Makarov pointwise bounds are not uniformly sharp for treatment-effect-distribution functionals; this motivates a joint transport envelope. FanPark2010 (Econometric Theory) supplies sharp Makarov-type treatment-effect-distribution bounds from marginals; gap is continuous-dose derivative signs. MadigasekaraPoskittZhangZhao2024 (arXiv, https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.04450) uses copula equality in panel DTE bounds; gap is adjacent-dose quantile-copula tubes under a continuous IV. BaiocchiChengSmall2023 (JRSS A, https://academic.oup.com/jrsssa/article/186/4/852/7198527) warns continuous-IV sensitivity models must incorporate dose information; this proposal keeps adjacent-dose rank information explicitly.
